title.en: FastAPI CRUD: Full API with Database

description.en: Build a complete CRUD API with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy async, database sessions, and proper error handling.
description.ta: FastAPI, SQLAlchemy async, தரவுத்தள அமர்வுகள் மற்றும் சரியான பிழை கையாளுதலுடன் முழுமையான CRUD API-ஐ உருவாக்குங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## The Full Kitchen

Vaathiyaar steps into a professional kitchen. "We've learned the ingredients — now we cook the full meal. A real API needs a **database**, **schema validation**, **error handling**, and **proper CRUD operations**. Let's build a production-quality API."

### The Stack
- **FastAPI** — Web framework
- **SQLAlchemy** — ORM (async mode)
- **Pydantic** — Request/response validation
- **SQLite/PostgreSQL** — Database

### CRUD Pattern in FastAPI

| Operation | HTTP | FastAPI Route | SQLAlchemy |
|-----------|------|---------------|------------|
| Create | POST | `@app.post('/items/')` | `db.add(item)` |
| Read All | GET | `@app.get('/items/')` | `db.execute(select(Item))` |
| Read One | GET | `@app.get('/items/{id}')` | `db.get(Item, id)` |
| Update | PUT | `@app.put('/items/{id}')` | `item.name = new` |
| Delete | DELETE | `@app.delete('/items/{id}')` | `db.delete(item)` |

### Dependency Injection
FastAPI uses `Depends()` to inject database sessions into routes:
```python
async def get_db():
    async with SessionLocal() as session:
        yield session

@app.get('/items/')
async def read_items(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    ...
```

> **Key Concept:** Dependency injection keeps your code clean. Instead of creating database connections inside each route, you declare what you need and FastAPI provides it.

steps.line:
# database.py — Database setup
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, DeclarativeBase

explanation.en: Set up the database engine and session factory

steps.line:
DATABASE_URL = 'sqlite:///./app.db'
engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL, connect_args={'check_same_thread': False})
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

explanation.en: SessionLocal creates new database sessions for each request
Base class for all SQLAlchemy models

steps.line:
# models.py — Database models
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, Boolean
from database import Base

explanation.en: SQLAlchemy models define database table structure

steps.line:
class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(500), default='')
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    in_stock = Column(Boolean, default=True)

explanation.en: Define the Item table with typed columns

steps.line:
# schemas.py — Pydantic models
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class ItemCreate(BaseModel):
    name: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=100)
    description: str = ''
    price: float = Field(..., gt=0)
    in_stock: bool = True

explanation.en: Pydantic schema for CREATE requests — validates input

steps.line:
class ItemUpdate(BaseModel):
    name: str | None = None
    description: str | None = None
    price: float | None = Field(default=None, gt=0)
    in_stock: bool | None = None

explanation.en: Update schema — all fields optional for partial updates
from_attributes=True allows reading from SQLAlchemy model attributes

steps.line:
# main.py — FastAPI app
from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from database import SessionLocal, engine, Base
from models import Item
from schemas import ItemCreate, ItemUpdate, ItemResponse

explanation.en: Import all components into the main application file
Dependency: yields a DB session, guarantees cleanup with finally

steps.line:
@app.post('/items/', response_model=ItemResponse, status_code=201)
def create_item(item: ItemCreate, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    db_item = Item(**item.model_dump())
    db.add(db_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

explanation.en: CREATE: validate with Pydantic, save with SQLAlchemy, return refreshed object
READ ALL: pagination with skip and limit query params
READ ONE: get by ID or raise 404
UPDATE: only change fields that were actually sent in the request
DELETE: remove the item and return 204 No Content

instruction.en: Build a complete CRUD API for a 'Todo' resource with fields: title, description, is_done, priority. Include database setup, Pydantic schemas, and all five endpoints (create, list, get, update, delete). Add a custom endpoint GET /todos/pending that returns only incomplete todos.
instruction.ta: title, description, is_done, priority புலங்களுடன் 'Todo' வளத்திற்கான முழுமையான CRUD API-ஐ உருவாக்குங்கள்.

en: Use Depends(get_db) to inject the database session

q.en: What does Depends(get_db) do in a FastAPI route?

en: Injects a database session into the route function

q.en: Why use model_dump(exclude_unset=True) for update operations?
q.ta: புதுப்பிப்பு செயல்பாடுகளுக்கு model_dump(exclude_unset=True) ஏன் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்?

en: To only update fields that were explicitly sent in the request
ta: கோரிக்கையில் வெளிப்படையாக அனுப்பப்பட்ட புலங்களை மட்டும் புதுப்பிக்க

q.en: What HTTP status code should DELETE return on success?
q.ta: வெற்றிகரமாக நீக்கும்போது DELETE எந்த HTTP நிலை குறியீட்டை திருப்ப வேண்டும்?
